instruction: How do I check the CPU temperature on a Linux server?
output: You can check CPU temperature using: (1) 'sensors' command - install lm-sensors package first. (2) Check /sys/class/thermal/thermal_zone*/temp. (3) Use 'watch sensors' for real-time monitoring. (4) Check BIOS/IPMI for hardware sensors. Normal range is 30-50°C idle, up to 80-90°C under load.

instruction: What are the best practices for managing disk space in a datacenter?
output: Disk space management best practices: (1) Monitor usage with 'df -h' and 'du -sh /*'. (2) Set up automated alerts at 80-85%. (3) Implement log rotation (logrotate) with 7-30 day retention. (4) Use LVM snapshots for efficient storage. (5) Archive old data to NAS/S3. (6) Regular cleanup of tmp files and caches. (7) Implement quotas per user/application.

instruction: How do I optimize network bandwidth in a datacenter environment?
input: Network latency increased during peak hours
output: Network optimization strategies: (1) Use iftop to identify bandwidth-heavy processes. (2) Implement QoS (Quality of Service) policies. (3) Enable TCP window scaling and optimize MTU size. (4) Use compression for data transfer when applicable. (5) Implement load balancing across network interfaces. (6) Monitor with tools like vnstat, iptraf. (7) Consider CDN or edge caching for frequently accessed data.

instruction: What is the procedure for gracefully shutting down a server?
output: Graceful server shutdown procedure: (1) Notify running services and users 30 minutes before. (2) Stop non-critical services: 'systemctl stop <service>'. (3) Check active connections: 'netstat -an' or 'ss -an'. (4) Flush caches: 'sync' command. (5) Initiate shutdown: 'shutdown -h +5' for scheduled shutdown. (6) Use 'wall' command to notify logged-in users. (7) Monitor logs during shutdown for errors.

instruction: How do I configure SSH key-based authentication for remote access?
output: SSH key-based authentication setup: (1) Generate key pair: ssh-keygen -t ed25519. (2) Copy public key to server: ssh-copy-id user@host. (3) Disable password auth in sshd_config: PasswordAuthentication no. (4) Set correct permissions: ~/.ssh 700, authorized_keys 600. (5) Use ssh-agent for key management. (6) Consider using bastion hosts for multi-hop access. (7) Rotate keys every 1-2 years.

instruction: What tools should I use for datacenter monitoring and alerting?
output: Recommended monitoring stack for datacenter: (1) Prometheus - metrics collection and storage. (2) Grafana - visualization and dashboards. (3) AlertManager - alert management. (4) Node Exporter - hardware metrics. (5) ELK Stack - centralized logging. (6) Zabbix - enterprise monitoring. (7) Datadog/New Relic - SaaS solutions. (8) Setup alerts for: CPU >80%, Memory >85%, Disk >90%, Network errors.

instruction: How do I handle server high temperature issues?
output: High temperature troubleshooting: (1) Immediate actions: reduce load, disable non-critical services. (2) Check airflow: ensure proper ventilation around server. (3) Clean dust filters and heatsinks. (4) Verify BIOS settings - check fan curves. (5) Check thermal paste condition. (6) Monitor with 'sensors' in real-time. (7) If persistent: reduce CPU frequency or consider hardware replacement. (8) Implement emergency shutdown at 100°C.

instruction: How do I manage SSL/TLS certificates on multiple servers?
output: SSL/TLS certificate management at scale: (1) Use Let's Encrypt for automated renewal. (2) Deploy Certbot with renewal automation. (3) Use configuration management tools: Ansible, Puppet, Chef. (4) Create certificate inventory spreadsheet tracking expiration dates. (5) Setup monitoring alerts 30 days before expiration. (6) Use ACME protocol for automated validation. (7) Implement certificate pinning where applicable. (8) Regular audit of certificate installation across fleet.

instruction: What is the recommended backup strategy for mission-critical data?
output: Mission-critical backup strategy: (1) Implement 3-2-1 rule: 3 copies, 2 different media, 1 offsite. (2) Use incremental backups for efficiency: daily incremental + weekly full. (3) Test restore procedures monthly. (4) Encrypt backups at rest and in transit. (5) Use rsync, bacula, or enterprise backup solutions. (6) Implement backup verification checksums. (7) Document RTO/RPO (Recovery Time/Point Objectives). (8) Maintain separate backup credentials.

instruction: How do I troubleshoot high memory usage on a server?
output: High memory troubleshooting: (1) Check process memory: 'ps aux --sort=-%mem'. (2) Use 'free -h' to see memory breakdown. (3) Check page cache with 'vmstat'. (4) Review application logs for leaks. (5) Use valgrind or top to identify culprits. (6) Check for zombie processes. (7) Implement memory limits using cgroups/systemd. (8) Upgrade RAM if consistently over 80% under normal load.

instruction: What is the procedure for adding a new server to a datacenter?
output: New server deployment checklist: (1) Install OS from PXE/USB with kickstart/preseed. (2) Configure networking: static IPs, DNS, gateway. (3) Set hostname and timezone. (4) Install essential packages and security updates. (5) Configure SSH, firewall, SELinux/AppArmor. (6) Add to monitoring system. (7) Join to domain/LDAP if applicable. (8) Run baseline security hardening. (9) Document in DCIM (hardware inventory system). (10) Add to configuration management system.

instruction: How do I implement redundancy for critical services?
output: Service redundancy implementation: (1) Implement N+1 or N+2 redundancy model. (2) Use load balancers (HAProxy, nginx) for health checks. (3) Configure automatic failover with Keepalived. (4) Use database replication for stateful services. (5) Implement shared storage or distributed filesystems (NFS, GlusterFS). (6) Setup heartbeat monitoring between nodes. (7) Test failover procedures quarterly. (8) Monitor active-passive transitions for issues.

instruction: How do I optimize database performance in a datacenter?
output: Database performance optimization: (1) Profile slow queries: enable slow query log. (2) Create indexes on frequently searched columns. (3) Use EXPLAIN to analyze query execution plans. (4) Implement connection pooling (pgBouncer, ProxySQL). (5) Enable query caching where appropriate. (6) Consider read replicas for scaling read-heavy workloads. (7) Implement sharding for large datasets. (8) Monitor with tools like New Relic or DataDog.

instruction: What are the key metrics to monitor for server health?
output: Essential server health metrics: (1) CPU usage - average and peak. (2) Memory - used, cached, available. (3) Disk - usage percentage, I/O latency. (4) Network - throughput, packet loss, errors. (5) Process count and zombie processes. (6) Load average - 1, 5, 15 minute intervals. (7) TCP connections - established, TIME_WAIT. (8) System logs - errors, warnings. (9) Uptime and reboot frequency. (10) Service-specific metrics for applications.

instruction: How do I handle RAID configuration and recovery?
output: RAID management and recovery: (1) Identify failed disk: check /proc/mdstat. (2) Hot-swap process: remove failed drive, insert new one. (3) Add new disk to array: mdadm --add /dev/md0 /dev/sdX1. (4) Monitor rebuild: watch -n 1 cat /proc/mdstat. (5) Verify with: mdadm --detail /dev/md0. (6) Keep spare drives available for automatic replacement. (7) Rebuild time depends on size (hours to days). (8) Never operate with multiple missing disks.

instruction: What is the best approach for security patching in production?
output: Production security patching strategy: (1) Establish patch windows: scheduled maintenance times. (2) Test patches in staging environment first. (3) Create system snapshots/backups before patching. (4) Use configuration management for consistent deployment. (5) Implement staged rollout: dev → staging → prod. (6) Monitor for regressions after patching. (7) For kernel patches: schedule reboot during maintenance window. (8) Keep audit trail of all patches applied. (9) Subscribe to security mailing lists for notifications.

instruction: How do I configure and manage firewall rules?
output: Firewall configuration best practices: (1) Implement default-deny policy: allow only necessary traffic. (2) Document all firewall rules in wiki/spreadsheet. (3) Use firewalld or ufw for rule management. (4) Segment network by zone: DMZ, internal, management. (5) Setup intrusion detection with Snort/Suricata. (6) Review rules quarterly for obsolete entries. (7) Implement stateful inspection. (8) Log all denied traffic for analysis. (9) Test rules with tools like nmap and netcat. (10) Automate rule deployment with Ansible.

instruction: What is the procedure for decommissioning a server?
input: End of life for 10 older servers
output: Server decommissioning procedure: (1) Backup all data and migrate services to new hardware. (2) Notify stakeholders of transition plan. (3) Verify no active connections or running services. (4) Remove from monitoring and configuration management. (5) Remove from DNS and DHCP. (6) Perform secure data erasure: 'shred' or DBAN. (7) Remove from DCIM system. (8) Document retirement in asset management. (9) Physically remove from racks. (10) Recycle or securely dispose per policy.
